disclaimer: Visa thresholds change frequently in the UAE. Verify the current ICP rules before applying. Recent changes captured: Golden Visa specialized-talent salary tightened to AED 30,000 basic monthly verified over 24 months. Federal health insurance mandate kicked in January 2025. Visa stickers discontinued since 2022 (residency is digitally tied to Emirates ID).

visas.name: Tourist Visa (30 days)
eligibility: Most nationalities require visa pre-arrival
Visa-on-arrival nationalities exempt
visas.process: Online via ICP/GDRFA portal, hotel sponsor, or airline.

visas.name: Investor / Partner Visa
best_for: anyone with a UAE trade license
eligibility: Active UAE trade license
Either mainland (DET) or free zone
Sponsor is the founder's own company
process_steps: Residence visa stamping (now digital, no sticker since 2022)

visas.name: Employment Visa
best_for: salaried role with a UAE employer
eligibility: Valid job offer from UAE-registered employer
Employer holds an active MOHRE quota or free zone equivalent

visas.name: Golden Visa (Specialized Talent)
eligibility: AED 30,000 minimum BASIC monthly salary, verified for the prior 24 months (tightened in early 2026)
Recognised qualification in the relevant field
Specialised in a priority field (medicine, engineering, scientific research, AI, etc.)
visas.process: Apply via ICP portal or Amer / GDRFA Dubai. Self-sponsored, no employer needed.

visas.name: Golden Visa (Real Estate Investor)
best_for: property investors with AED 2M+ in UAE real estate
eligibility: Property worth AED 2 million or more (single or combined)
Property must be paid off (or substantial equity in mortgaged property)
Property in approved areas

visas.name: Golden Visa (Entrepreneur)
best_for: founders with technical or future fields project
eligibility: Project worth at least AED 500,000
Technical or future-fields project (approved by relevant authority)
Approval from accredited business incubator OR Ministry of Economy

visas.name: Green Visa (Skilled Employee)
best_for: skilled employees with a valid MOHRE work permit
eligibility: Valid MOHRE work permit
MOHRE skill level 1, 2, or 3
Bachelor's degree or equivalent
AED 15,000 minimum monthly salary
visas.process: Self-sponsored after obtaining a qualifying employment record.

visas.name: Green Visa (Freelancer / Self-Employed)
eligibility: Freelance / self-employed permit from MOHRE
Bachelor's degree or specialized diploma
AED 360,000 cumulative income over the prior two years OR proof of financial solvency for the residency duration
visas.process: Self-sponsored. Requires MOHRE freelance permit first.

visas.name: Freelance Permit (TECOM / GoFreelance)
best_for: solo professionals in tech, media, education, design
eligibility: Recognised qualification or portfolio in a permitted activity
Application via TECOM, GoFreelance, or other free zone freelance program

visas.name: Virtual Working Programme
best_for: remote employees of foreign companies who want UAE residency
eligibility: Employment contract with a foreign (non-UAE) employer
USD 3,500 minimum monthly salary
Health insurance valid in the UAE

visas.name: Retirement Visa
best_for: age 55 and older with assets or income
eligibility: Age 55 or older
AED 1 million property OR savings, OR AED 20,000 monthly income

visas.name: Family / Dependent Visa
best_for: spouse and children of UAE residents
eligibility: Sponsor (the UAE resident) holds a valid residence visa
Sponsor minimum salary AED 4,000 plus housing OR AED 5,000 all-in
Attested marriage and birth certificates
